data.title: Digi Power X Inc. reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Digi Power X Inc. press release (DGX:CA): Q3  Achieved positive net income of $0.3 million (Q3 2024: -$6.4 million), EBITDA* of $1.9 million (Q3 2024: -$2.5 million) and Adjusted EBITDA* of $0.8 million (Q3 2024: -$0.96 million) in Q3 2025, representing sustained profitability and efficiency of operations.
  • Strong Working Capital Position – Significant balance sheet improvements led to a working capital position of $15.1 million at the end of Q3 2025 (Q3 2024: $0.5 million), representing an increase of 2,731% over the same quarter of the prior year.

data.title: Lowe's Q3 results bolstered by professional segment but still issues cautious FY25 guidance
data.description:
In anticipation that professional demand will compensate for slowing sales in the do-it-yourself category, Lowe’s (LOW) raised its full year sales guidance to a level above Wall Street’s expectations, offsetting mixed third quarter results and driving shares higher in Wednesday’s premarket trading.

“With the closing of the [Foundation Building Materials] acquisition last month, we look forward to enhancing our offering to Pro customers and creating more sustainable, long-term sales and profit expansion for the company," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman, president and CEO.

The company now sees FY25 sales at $86.0B versus earlier guidance of $84.5B to $85.5B and exceeding $85.66B estimates.

However, the rest of the outlook is more subdued. Management now expects comparable-store sales to be flat, down from prior guidance of flat to up 1% and below the +0.32% consensus. Adjusted operating margin was also cut to 12.1% from 12.2%–12.3%, missing the 12.2% estimate.

The company’s profit is now expected to be approximately $12.25 per share from an earlier range of $12.20 to $12.45 per share, and 2 cents less than anticipated.

These estimates include the “ongoing uncertainty in the macroeconomic environment and expectations for FBM.”

Similar to rival Home Depot (HD) – which also issued cautious guidance for FY25 -- Lowe’s reported mixed results for the third quarter. Both reported a very modest increase in sales with Lowe’s 3.2% increase falling short of estimates by $30M. Unlike Home Depot (HD), Lowe’s (LOW) profitability improved with adjusted earnings of $3.06 up 5.9% year-over-year and 11 cents better than expected.

Comparable store sales at Lowe’s (LOW) were up just 0.4% -- versus +1.02% estimates -- driven by continued growth in its Pro segment, while Home Depot’s (HD) comparable store sales were up just 0.2%, also attributing the majority of the boost to professional sales.

The boost in Lowe’s (LOW) shares is also giving a lift to Home Depot (HD), currently up nearly 1% in Wednesday’s premarket after closing with a loss of 6% on Tuesday.

data.title: China Gold International Resources reports Q3 results
data.description:
• China Gold International Resources  press release (CGG:CA): Q3 Revenue of $345M.

  • Net profit of US$142.3 million increased by US$114.4 million from US$27.9 million for the same period in 2024, achieving new records for the highest quarterly net profit in two consecutive quarters. 

  • Cash flow from operation of US$234.7 million, increased from US$156.2 million for the same period in 2024. 

  • Total gold production decreased by 18% to 41,150 ounces from 50,160 ounces for the same period in 2024. 

  • Total copper production was 42.3 million pounds (approximately 19,204 tonnes) an increase from 38.3 million pounds (approximately 17,361 tonnes) for the same period in 2024.

data.title: Viking's bigger fleet, increased capacity helps with Q3 results beat
data.description:
Cruise operator Viking Holdings (VIK) reported a top-and-bottom-line beat for the third quarter. Shares of the company are seesawing between green and red in premarket trading on Wednesday; VIK stock was down 2% but pared those losses and is currently up about 2%.

The company saw revenue growth of 19% in Q3 and generated about $2B, which was ahead of the consensus estimate by $10M, driven by increased capacity passenger cruise days and higher occupancy, among other things.

Capacity PCDs in Q3 rose by 11%, driven by the growth of the company’s fleet. The rate of occupancy was 96%.

Adjusted EBITDA climbed nearly 27% to $703.5M and was ahead of the consensus estimate of $680M.

Vessel operating expenses jumped 19.1%, and 21.7% excluding fuel, due to the increase in the size of the company’s fleet in 2025 compared to 2024.

For its core products, the company sold 96% of its capacity PCDs as of November 2 for the 2025 season.

"Looking ahead, we continue to see strong momentum across our product offering, with 70% of the 2026 capacity for our Core Products already sold," CEO Torstein Hagen said.

Net income for the quarter was $514M, or $1.15 per share, compared to $379.7M, or $0.87 per share, in the same period last year.

On an adjusted basis, the company earned $1.20 per share, managing to beat estimates by a cent.

data.title: Extreme Networks initiated with Buy rating at BofA
data.description:
• Extreme Networks (EXTR) was initiated with a Buy rating and $24 price target at Bank of America on Wednesday.
  • Shares rose 3.7% in premarket trading.
  • “Catalysts for the stock include a WiFi-7 upgrade cycle, Campus refresh/modernization initiatives, and growing adoption of Cloud and SaaS-based offerings driving our estimates moderately above Street,” analyst Tomer Zilberman wrote in a note to clients. “Our PO is based on roughly 17x CY27E P/E, a slight discount to the networking peer average of 18x, which is justified by its smaller operating scale and greater exposure to budget-sensitive verticals. Growing SaaS and Wi-Fi 7 contribution is a key catalyst Network modernization initiatives and the growing demand for AI-integrated infrastructure are accelerating the adoption of software and cloud-managed solutions. Extreme has been successfully transitioning to Cloud and SaaS-based offerings, with its ExtremeCloud IQ platform serving as the backbone for the Company’s cloud-based management solution, with Platform ONE serving as another pillar of long-term growth opportunity.”
  • Delving deeper, Zilberman said he expects Extreme Networks' software-as-a-service annual recurring revenue growth to accelerate to 24% in fiscal 2027 from 22% in fiscal 2026, thanks to increased adoption of its Platform ONE offering. “Moderate market share gains support outperformance We think the Street is underestimating Extreme’s growth potential,” Zilberman added.

data.title: Joby Aviation to conduct air taxi test flights in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Joby Aviation (JOBY) has partnered with Red Sea Global and The Helicopter Company ((THC)) to complete pre-commercial test flights of its electric air taxi in Saudi Arabia in the first half of 2026, paving the way for future air-taxi deployments in the Kingdom.

Under the memorandum of understanding, Joby will work together with RSG, the developer behind regenerative tourism destinations The Red Sea and AMAALA, and THC to establish a "sandbox" for pre-commercial operations.

The sandbox will serve as a base for the evaluation of potential air taxi operations in the region, with Joby conducting representative flights with charging, airspace integration, and ground communications, similar to the flights Joby completed in the UAE earlier this year.

The partnership comes on the heels of Joby's announcement last week that it would collaborate with the General Authority of Civil Aviation to support the development of the Kingdom's air taxi regulatory framework, using Federal Aviation Administration certification standards as a foundation.

JOBY +1.2% premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Agios falls after mixed data from late-stage trial for sickle cell therapy
data.description:
Shares of Agios Pharmaceuticals (AGIO) plunged ~45% in the premarket on Wednesday after the biotech announced a mixed outcome from a late-stage trial for its lead asset mitapivat in sickle cell disease, a rare, inherited blood disorder.

Cambridge, Massachusetts-based Agios (AGIO) noted that based on topline data, its RISE UP Phase 3 trial met its primary endpoint of hemoglobin response, with mitapivat indicating a statistically significant improvement compared to placebo.

However, there was no statistically significant reduction in the annualized rate of sickle cell pain crises, another primary endpoint, and the 207-patient trial also failed in a key secondary endpoint, the company said.

The placebo-controlled trial was designed to evaluate mitapivat, marketed as Pyrukynd, for anemia in adults with pyruvate kinase ((PK)) deficiency in patients aged 16 years or older with sickle cell disease.

While RISE UP demonstrated statistically significant improvements in the first two key secondary endpoints, 20.3% of patients on mitapivat experienced Serious treatment-emergent adverse events compared to 29.0% in the placebo arm.

Three deaths were reported in the on-drug arm compared to two in the control arm, while 4.3% on mitapivat discontinued the therapy due to TEAEs compared to 2.9% on placebo.

However, Agios (AGIO) said it would meet with the U.S. FDA in Q1 2026 and submit a marketing application for the oral pyruvate kinase activator in SCD.

The company added that it plans to cut expenses to maintain its solid financial profile and support the potential U.S. commercial rollout of Pyrukynd in thalassemia, and will issue an update in this regard early next year.

data.title: Dycom to acquire Power Solutions for $1.95B to boost data center infrastructure services
data.description:
• Dycom Industries (DY) said on Wednesday it has agreed to acquire Power Solutions, a major Mid-Atlantic electrical contractor serving data centers, in a deal valuing the company at $1.95 billion.
  • The acquisition will expand its presence in fast-growing digital and data-center infrastructure markets and add more than 2,800 skilled employees.
  • Power Solutions, based in Bowie, Maryland, is expected to generate about $1 billion in revenue in 2025 and has maintained mid-to-high-teens adjusted EBITDA margins, supported by a backlog exceeding $1 billion.

  • The purchase price includes about $293 million in Dycom stock, with the remainder paid in cash.

  • Dycom plans to finance the deal with cash on hand, a $1 billion term loan A facility and a $700 million bridge loan. The company expects pro forma net leverage to be below 3.0x at closing and to fall to around 2.0x within 12 to 18 months.

  • Power Solutions will continue to operate under its existing brand and management team.

  • Dycom said the deal will be immediately accretive to adjusted EBITDA margin and adjusted diluted EPS and improve free cash flow.

  • DY +8.67% premarket to $321.9
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Iron ore rises to two-week high on firm demand and reduced supply from China
data.description:
Iron ore futures rose to their highest in more than two weeks on Wednesday, helped by strong demand and declining domestic supply in top consumer China.

According to Reuters, the January contract for iron ore (SCO:COM) on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange closed daytime trading +0.7% at 791.5 yuan/metric ton ($111.32), after touching its highest level since November 3 at 797 yuan/ton, and benchmark December iron ore traded on the Singapore Exchange was +0.1% to $104.55/ton after hitting its highest since November 4 at $104.95/ton.

Chinese steel mills have used a low-inventory strategy for raw materials during the past three years as margins were squeezed by falling steel prices alongside the protracted property downturn, which analysts say means that mills will need to return to the spot market to restock cargoes more frequently, thus improving spot liquidity, which to some extent acted as a buffer during a price slump.

Lower domestic supply also is supporting iron ore prices, as official data released this week showed China's run-of mine - raw ore to be processed to concentrate and pellets - fell in October by 2.9% Y/Y to 84 million tons.

Potentially relevant stocks include BHP (BHP), Rio Tinto (RIO), Vale (VALE), Antofagasta (OTC:ANFGF), Glencore (CLNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) and Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY).
